claims.claim_text: telc Deutsch B1-B2 Pflege is a nursing-specific German exam whose certificate demonstrates reading, listening, writing and speaking competence at levels B1 and B2.
claims.notes: This is strong exam-route context, not proof that the proposed sprint is sufficient for most learners.

claims.claim_text: Goethe-Test PRO Pflege is a nursing-focused B2 proof route recognised by the BAMF and the German Foreign Office, and the overall B2 result requires all four exam parts to be passed at at least B2.
claims.notes: The safe framing is that this is a recognised B2 route; it does not by itself prove buyer acceptance of the product.

claims.claim_text: For regulated nursing work in Germany, German language skills at least at B2 are generally required; for nursing trainees from abroad, the required level can be B1 or B2 depending on the federal state.
claims.notes: This matters because the product title spans B1 to B2, but the public route is not one single universal threshold.

claims.claim_text: The Federal Employment Agency reported average nursing-sector vacancies of 35,000 in 2023 and only 44 jobseekers per 100 registered qualified nursing jobs.
claims.notes: This is the strongest current official shortage signal found in the rerun.

claims.claim_text: The Federal Employment Agency reported 270,000 foreign-national qualified nurses in Germany in 2023, equal to 16% of qualified nurses.
claims.notes: Keep the date attached to the figure. This is safer than using a timeless 300K+ claim.

claims.claim_text: By 9 May 2025, the Federal Employment Agency said the proportion of foreign workers in nursing professions was 18%.
claims.notes: This is a later percentage update, not proof of a 300K+ stock figure.

claims.claim_text: BAMF-administered occupational German support includes certificate courses and specialist-language-exam preparation that can support recognition of foreign qualifications, including in healthcare.
claims.notes: This proves the ecosystem route exists. It does not prove the B1 product itself is eligible or reimbursable.

claims.claim_text: Official German nursing recruitment routes include the Triple Win programme for nurses from India (Kerala and Telangana), Indonesia, the Philippines and Tunisia, with German-language and specialist nursing-course support.
claims.notes: This is public route context only. It does not prove that B1 is embedded in Triple Win or any official programme.

claims.claim_text: Bilateral agreements with India, Morocco and Brazil define the recruiting route.
claims.notes: The official nursing route names India, Indonesia, the Philippines and Tunisia. Brazil and Morocco appear on other public projects, not as the defining nursing route.

claims.claim_text: An 8-12 week sprint is enough for most nurses to reach the required route proof.
claims.notes: The rerun verifies route thresholds and exam types, but not product efficacy or median learner progress.

claims.claim_text: 300K+ foreign nurses already work in Germany (17.8%).
claims.notes: The safest public formulation is 270,000 and 16% in 2023, plus an 18% share in 2025. The combined 300K+ / 17.8% line did not survive.

claims.claim_text: No strong online-first healthcare German brand exists.
claims.notes: No exhaustive market scan was completed in this strict rerun, so the absence claim cannot be published.

claims.claim_text: The family-income framing is a reliable conversion lever.
claims.notes: This is a commercial hypothesis, not a public fact established in the rerun.
